name: Collective Ambition
text1: For: Purge all "Support for the Throne" promissory notes.
text2: Against: Players who voted "For" purge their "Alliance" promissory note, if able

name: Council Abolishment
text1: For: At the start of the agenda phase, each player exhausts half their planets, rounded up, at random.
text2: Against: Each player readies up to 2 of their planets, at random. Then, reveal 1 agenda from the top of the deck. Players vote on that agenda.
mapText: At the start of the agenda phase, each player exhausts half their planets, rounded up, at random.

name: Council Games
text1: For: Each player may place 1 infantry from their reinforcements into coexistence on a non-home planet another player controls.
text2: Against: Each player destroys all but 2 of their units in coexistence on each planet.

name: Galactic Fair
text1: For: Each player may research 1 technology that has no prerequisites.
text2: Against: Each player who voted "For" discards 1 of their technologies that has the fewest prerequisites.

name: Galactic Sanctions
text1: For: During the status phase, Initiative Order is reversed, and begins with the player who has the highest-numbered strategy card.
text2: Against: Each player who voted "For" may resolve the secondary ability of 1 strategy card of their choice.

name: Imperial Referendum
text1: For: Each player who has the fewest victory points gains 2 command tokens.
text2: Against: Each player who does not have the most victory points loses 1 command token from their command sheet.

name: Imperialist Edict
text1: For: When a player is eliminated, all remaining players gain 1 victory point.
text2: Against: Each player places 1 command token from their fleet pool in the Mecatol Rex system.
mapText: When a player is eliminated, all remaining players gain 1 victory point.

name: Invalidated Patent
text1: Each player gains the elected technology, if able

name: Isolationist Movement
text1: For: Players are not neighbors with another player unless they have units in the same system as that player.
text2: Against: Each player who voted "For" may produce 1 unit in a system that contains 1 or more of their ships and a planet they control.
mapText: Players are not neighbors with another player unless they have units in the same system as that player.

name: Minister of Diplomacy
text1: Attach that player's "Alliance" promissory note to this card, all players have that alliance ability.
mapText: All player's have the alliance ability of the elected player.

name: Minister of Justice
text1: For each player in the game, the elected player may gain 1 command token and allow that player to gain 1 command token.

name: Political Entanglement
text1: For: Each player with the most victory points gives 1 promissory note to 2 of their neighbours.
text2: Against: Each player with the fewest victory points may take 1 promissory note at random from 2 other players.

name: Scientific Collaboration
text1: For: Players also have the synergies of their neighbors.
text2: Against: Each player with synergy in any colors purges one of their unowned technologies in each of those colors.
mapText: Players have the synergies of their neighbors.

name: Strategic Coordination
text1: Attach this card to that strategy card. Players may resolve the secondary of this strategy card without spending a command token
mapText: May resolve the secondary of elected card without spending a command token.

name: Suspicious Vault
text1: For: Place 1 ingress token in the home system of each player that voted "For", if able. If the Fracture is not in play, the speaker places it in play.
text2: Against: Each player destroys 5 of their units in each system in the Fracture, and 3 units in each system not in, but adjacent to a system in, the Fracture.

name: Trade Monopoly
text1: For: During the action phase, the active player cannot resolve transactions unless they have 3 or more trade goods.
text2: Against: Place 2 neutral fighters in each system that contains no ships.
mapText: Active player cannot transact unless they have 3 trade goods.

name: Transparency Accord
text1: For: Each player reveals one of their unscored secret objectives, at random, if able.
text2: Against: Each player who voted "For" draws 1 secret objective.

name: Vote Of Confidence
text1: For: The speaker gains 1 victory point.
text2: Against: During the next strategy phase, strategy cards are chosen at random.
